entry.title: Error "Duplicate column names in the result" with columns from different tables (BigQuery DB)
entry.content:
Hello,

On WebI, I get an error when a query contains 2 or more columns with the same name but from different tables.

For example, in the following query:

SELECT
TABLE_1.COL_NAME
TABLE_2.COL_NAME
(...)

=> TABLE_1.COL_NAME and TABLE_2.COL_NAME are interpreted as duplicate coloumn names. I get the following error and the query cannot be executed either on Webi or the IDT query panel:

"The following database error occurred: [Simba][BigQuery] (70) Invalid query: Duplicate column names in the result are not supported when a destination table is present. Found duplicate(s): COL_NAME.
For information about this error, please refer to SAP Knowledge Base Article 2054721 on the SAP Support Portal. (IES 10901)"

There is no error when I run the query directly on BigQuery.

Environnement info:
BO version: 4.3 SP4 Patch 6
connection: native ODBC BigQuery driver

As a workaround, I add an alias in the SQL definition of the objets in the universe. Unfortunatly, this cannot be a sustainable solution.


Note: The problem did not occur on our previous platform BO 4.2 SP9 Patch 7 using a generic ODBC driver (which is not supported by SAP).

Thank you in advance

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh Blog Collection
entry.content:
Introduction

In today’s fast-paced, event-driven world, seamless data movement and real-time responsiveness have become critical to modern application architectures. SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh (AEM) offers a powerful solution—enabling dynamic, reliable, and scalable event streaming across hybrid and multi-cloud environments.

Over time, we've created a substantial amount of content around Advanced Event Mesh—now it makes sense to bring all the pieces together in one place as a cohesive blog collection, including Guides & contributions from other AEM Experts in the Community.

This blog serves as a central hub—a curated collection of insightful articles, technical deep dives and best practices focused on AEM and SAP Event Enablement Options like RAP, AIFAEM and the SAP Event-Enablement Add-On (ASAPIO). Whether you're just getting started or looking to optimize a production-grade deployment, this collection is designed to guide you through the concepts and capabilities of AEM.

Let’s explore how event mesh technology is transforming integration strategies and empowering developers and architects to build the next generation of event-driven systems.

 

Getting Started

Get Started with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

Advanced Event Mesh: Basic Concepts 

Advanced Event Mesh: Create your first event broker

 

AEM Features

SAP Advanced Event Mesh – Manage Advance Event Mesh with API’s 

Automating Advanced Event Mesh Broker Creation 

Advanced Event Mesh: Replay

Advanced Event Mesh: Queue 

Enabling In-Order processing with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh Partitioned Queues

Securing SAP Advanced Event Mesh: OAuth Configuration and SEMP API Integration

 

Connectivity & Auth

Advanced Event Mesh: Client Authentication - Basic / OAuth 

Advanced Event Mesh Connectors and Easy Event-Driven Example of S/4HANA with Amazon S3 Integration

 

Event Portal / Developer Hub

Enable SAP Developer Hub as central Event Catalog

Discover S/4HANA Cloud Events in SAP Developer Hub

 

Monitoring

Configure SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh Distributed Tracing

Evaluate SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh Datadog Distributed Tracing

Advanced Event Mesh:Troubleshooting

 

User Management

Introduction to Group Management in SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh

Streamlining User Management in Advanced Event Mesh with SAP CIS and Identity Federation

SAP Advanced Event Mesh – Event Access Approval Workflow 

 

Deployment Options

Setting Up a Private SAP Advanced Event Mesh Cluster on SUSE Rancher RKE2

 

Kafka Bridge

SAP Integration Suite – Connect Everything FEATURING Kafka

Bridging Advanced Event Mesh and Kafka: Dynamic Topics and Headers in Action

 

SAP Event Enablement

RAP

RAP Business Events with SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh and SAP S/4HANA On Premise 2023

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [1]: Connecting AEM to S/4HANA On Premise 2023

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [2]: Creating custom business events

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [3]: Creating events with deep payloads

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [4]: Creating a derived event

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [5]: Consuming a local business event

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [6]: Consuming a remote event

 

AIFAEM

A Natural Choice: SAP AIF can now expose events to Advanced Event Mesh to power your EDA 

How to set up enhanced Events for Advanced Event Mesh with Application Interface Framework & AIFAEM

How to send out IDocs to Advanced Event Mesh using SAP Application Interface Framework & AIFAEM

How to set up Filters in AIFAEM

How to Display the JSON Payload in AIFAEM

How to send Custom HTTP Headers in AIFAEM

 

Initial Loads

Blog Series - Intitial Loads to SAP Advanced Event Mesh

 

Other

BTP Event Mesh Advance Concepts

 

Summary

This collection brings together key insights and technical deep dives around Advanced Event Mesh to help you navigate, implement, and optimize event-driven architectures. If you notice that a relevant blog post or important topic is missing, feel free to let me know in the comments—I'm happy to expand the list and keep this collection as complete and useful as possible.

entry.title: ABAP News for SAP BTP ABAP Environment 2505
entry.content:
In this blog, you'll learn about the new features for SAP BTP ABAP Environment 2505 that were documented in the ABAP keyword documentation and ABAP data models documentation.

News for ABAP Release 9.15 

ABAP CDS

CDS Delegated Buffers

Delegated buffers in CDS are view entity buffers that delegate the buffer usage to the buffer of the base object. This saves memory in the AS ABAP. To turn buffer delegation on or off, use the annotation:

@AbapCatalog.entityBuffer.definitionType:[#DELEGATED]

To define a delegated buffer, use the syntax:

DEFINE VIEW ENTITY BUFFER ON cds_entity … TYPE DELEGATED

More about CDS delegated buffers

ABAP SQL

Level-based Hierarchies

The hierarchy function in ABAP SQL can now create an ABAP SQL hierarchy as a level-based hierarchy. Level-based hierarchies allow access to hierarchical data in data sources defined by level columns. To define a level-based hierarchy, use the addition LEVELS:

... HIERARCHY( SOURCE data_source ...
               LEVELS ( col1, col2,... )
               ... ) ...

More about level-based hierarchies

ABAP Keyword Documentation

Starting with this release, we will publish the ABAP Keyword Documentation content with the UI5 design. With the UI5 design, the documentation is even more aligned with SAP's corporate design. The functionality of the ADT design remains, and you can still search with the type-ahead functionality and send links. In addition, you can now copy code snippets and switch between syntax variants by clicking on tabs. In the header section, you can click the News button to know what's new in the current release or switch to an earlier one.

More about the UI5 design

Test and Consume CDS Data Models

The ABAP Data Models documentation is the home of two new sections: Test and Consume.

  • In Test, you find information about how to develop automated tests for CDS entities. Currently, unit tests for CDS entities are covered, using the ABAP CDS test double framework. Additionally, the AMDP test double framework has been integrated into ABAP for unit testing CDS table functions.
  • In Consume, you find information on the local consumption and service consumption of CDS data models.

More about Test
More about Consume

New Terms

The following new terms are used.

TermDefinition
RAP event-driven side effectA RAP side effect that is triggered by a business event for side effects. The business event in question is defined to be used exclusively for a side effect with the syntax event MyEvent for side effects.
RAP change documents

An optional part of an entity behavior definition that enables change logging of persisted RAP BO data for a change documents-enabled RAP BO entity. When enabled, changes are written to a change document object. RAP change documents are a RAP reuse service.
CDS table entity
 
A CDS persistent entity that defines a database table in ABAP CDS. A table entity is defined as a CDS data definition using the statement DEFINE TABLE ENTITY.
writable database objectA Repository object that allows modifying data on the database.
  • In ABAP CDS, CDS table entities, writable CDS view entities, and writable CDS external entities allow for the modification of data with the ABAP SQL statements INSERT, UPDATE, MODIFY, and DELETE.
  • In ABAP Dictionary, DDIC database tables allow data change operations.
writable CDS view entityA CDS view entity that allows modifying data on the database using the ABAP SQL INSERT, UPDATE, MODIFY, and DELETE operations. Defined using the statement WRITABLE.
writable CDS external entityA dynamic external entity that allows modifying data on an external database system using the ABAP SQL INSERT, UPDATE, MODIFY, and DELETE operations. Defined using the statement WRITABLE in the CDS data definition. An ABAP SQL connection object is required to establish a service connection and enable the logical external schema for write access.
ABAP SQL connection objectAn  instance of a class that implements IF_ABAP_SQL_CONNECTION and represents a valid database connection.
level-based hierarchyAn  ABAP SQL hierarchy that is generated from a SQL data source where certain columns define from left to right the hierarchy levels and where each row content of these columns represents a path from a start node to a leaf node that spans these levels. In a level-based hierarchy, all leaf nodes have the same level that is the highest level defined in the data source. For each leaf node, a similar set of ancestor nodes is generated for the lower levels of the data source. In ABAP SQL, the addition LEVELS of the hierarchy generator HIERARCHY can be used to generate level-based hierarchies.
parent-child hierarchyA hierarchy, where the hierarchy edges are parent-child relationships.

If you have any questions, feel free to comment below. We hope you enjoy the new features.

entry.title: Work Order and Notification Required Start/End Dates not synchronized according to Priority config
entry.content:
Priority type is configured and assigned to the Work Order and Notification.  When I created an emergency work order where the notification is auto-generated, I noticed a discrepancy in the Required Start and end date of the 2 documents.  Example P1 - 1 to 7 days ( config - Rel Start =1; Rel End = 7;  Unit = day).  

In Work Order:  Required Start = 05/28/2025 (mm-dd-yyyy); Required End = 06/05/2025 (mm-dd-yyyy)

In Notification: Require Start = 05/27/2025 ; Required End = 06/02/2025

In summary, the Notification dates are correct because the range is 7 days and it started on the date of creation itself. Whereas the Work Order required start is on the following day.

entry.title: Taking Flight with SAP: Marco's Journey to SAP Champion
entry.content:
“In a world that is constantly reinventing itself, I enjoy collecting and passing on knowledge and always thinking one step ahead.”  That mindset has guided Marco Büscher from his early days supporting flight operations in the military to becoming a highly skilled SAP consultant and solution architect.  

Over the past 15 years, his drive to understand how various business processes fit together has led him to master multiple SAP solutions. What started as a curiosity has evolved into expertise, and Marco now thrives in the dynamic world of business informatics, constantly learning and growing, both as a professional and a mentor. 

Marco’s SAP journey began after his time in the German Armed Forces, where he gained invaluable insights into flight operations. It was there he realized the importance of understanding complex systems from all angles, a lesson he later applied to his work with SAP software. He first encountered SAP solutions in his professional life as a business IT specialist, quickly recognizing how the system’s many modules could integrate to manage business processes effectively. 

A Personal Learning Sabbatical 

Over the years, Marco’s curiosity pushed him to continually expand his knowledge. “I received my first certification in 2009, when SAP S/4HANA was still a long way off,” he recalls. “Over the years, SAP has reinvented itself again and again”. To keep up with the evolving technology, he decided to take what he calls a “learning sabbatical”. He used this time to focus on deepening his expertise in SAP solutions and acquiring multiple SAP Certifications in ABAP and SAP S/4HANA solutions.  

“My certifications form the basis for my work as a consultant and technical architect at NTT DATA Deutschland SE,” he explains. “I utilize this experience to lead the way as a pioneer, provide guidance as a navigator and offer promising solutions as a designer in a complex and ever-changing landscape”.  

Marco credits SAP Learning Hub for providing clear, well-structured, guided learning resources that helped him to get and stay certified: “The learning journey was the best way for me to learn SAP,” he adds. From his perspective, upskilling and training is the only way to unlock the full potential of SAP software. 

"By acquiring knowledge in all key SAP solutions and combining it with my understanding of the system from a developer’s perspective, I am now able to satisfy customer requirements in both standard and customized SAP solutions”. 

A Leader and Mentor in the SAP Community 

Marco’s journey didn’t stop at mastering the technical aspects of SAP. Today, he is not only an expert in his field but also a mentor, sharing his knowledge and experiences with others who are looking to build their careers in SAP.  Through his active involvement in the SAP Community, particularly his participation in SAP CodeJams, he has become recognized as an SAP Champion, a role that fills him with pride.  

As he continues to guide others, he’s not just solving complex problems—he’s helping them see the bigger picture and empowering them to chart their own success in SAP. 

The Power of Continuous Learning 

Marco’s story is a testament to the power of continuous learning and self-improvement. His curiosity, commitment, and the willingness to learn new things has led him to embrace the full potential of SAP and seek out opportunities for growth. In doing so, Marco has not only shaped his own career but is also making a lasting impact on others, as is the goal of every SAP Champion. 

Accelerate your Career Today 

You too can benefit from tailored and self-paced learning opportunities that meet you when and where you learn best on SAP Learning site. Get certified to demonstrate your expertise and boost your career.

entry.title: Re: Interview Scheduling for Mass Recruitment
entry.content:
Hi @Kahley ,

Below are some more targeted suggestions and workarounds specifically for SuccessFactors Recruiting – Interview Scheduling for mass hiring.

1. Review the Interview Scheduling URL Format
Sometimes the issue may be due to malformed or incomplete links generated for certain candidates.

Validate the email template tokens: Go to Admin Center > Manage Recruiting Email Templates and confirm the correct tokens are being used for the interview scheduling link (e.g., [[INTERVIEW_SCHEDULING_URL]] or similar).

2. Use the “Interview Central” Alternative
Instead of mass emailing via the standard interview invitation, you can try a workaround:

Use Interview Central to manually assign slots if the system isn't handling mass scheduling well.

If automation is a must, consider splitting candidate lists into smaller batches (e.g., 10 at a time) — SuccessFactors sometimes has issues processing bulk updates or notifications.

3. Use “Interview Scheduling Assistant” Feature
This is a newer tool designed to improve scheduling.

Go to Admin Center > Manage Recruiting Settings, and ensure the Interview Scheduling Assistant is enabled.

It provides improved calendar views, integration with Microsoft Outlook/Google Calendar, and sometimes resolves page loading issues.

4. Check SuccessFactors Integration with MS Outlook / Google Calendar
Many "blank page" errors stem from integration or permission issues:

Ensure ICS file generation is functioning and that linked calendar systems (e.g., Office 365 or Google Workspace) are correctly integrated and authenticated in Admin Center > Configure Interview Scheduling.

Check if the interviewers' calendars are shared properly. If the system can’t access them, it may generate a broken or blank scheduling page.

Since you already submitted a ticket:

Escalate the ticket by referencing the number of affected candidates and hiring deadlines.

Attach screenshots, HAR files, and test links from affected candidates.

Request replication support — SAP may offer temporary hotfixes for known issues, especially if they can reproduce it in your instance.

Hope this helps!!

Thanks and Regards,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: ODP MODIFY DELTA DATE CLIENT EXTRACTOR
entry.content:
Hi

 

It is not supported to make manual changes in any administration tables. Especially not in tables that handles the delta pointer.

ODP supports the repeat of the last delta (just like the normal SAPI), yet there is no standard way to manipulate the subscriptions and they should not be touched at all.

If you need to load a specific data set, use a full load with a specific filter on the neede data.

 

br

Roland

entry.title: Output File Name changed in upper case automatically for program FAGL_CL_REGROUP
entry.content:
Hello,

 

We want to define Output File Name on program FAGL_CL_REGROUP, but the location path entered in lower case is automatically changed to upper case.

 

The problem is that upper case location is not readable on linux OS.

 

So instead having that : /apps/K_NICE/userdata/testspool

 

we have this location: /APPS/K_NICE/USERDATA/TESTSPOOL

entry.title: Discrepancy Analysis of Cross-currency Balances
entry.content:
First, open the application named "Display Customer Summary China".

 

Next, enter the query conditions to check the balance for December 2024, and it shows that the USD balance is 0.

 

Then, enter the query conditions again to look for the data of January 2025, and the initial balance of USD is displayed as 51.06.

 

 
It can be seen from the above that the balance at the end of December 2024 is inconsistent with the balance at the beginning of January 2025. After further analysis, it is found that the discrepancy is caused by the clearing of accounts between USD and CNY. However, this type of business is a normal operation. The problem is that the current report system is not capable of querying transaction currency balances across different currencies.

entry.title: Which Client to operate "SAP Access Control for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in", 000 or ?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

this is a question about Client 000 and SAP Access Control for S/4HANA Private Edition.

Introduction & Background:

SAP Access Control for S/4HANA Private Edition documentation is here: 

SAP Access Control for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition 

and this documentation points to

-> Digital Library

which points to

-> SAP Access Control | SAP Help Portal

I don't see any recommendation or best practice in the documentation regarding which Client to operate Access Control in, and whether or not to use the default Client 000.

Question:

When we install the Access Control "System", the Access Control functionality by default is running in Client 000.

With SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, the Customer is restricted on what they can do in Client 000 as SAP ECS own and manage Client  000  

SAP_HANA_USER_MANAGEMENT_ABAP_Client_000.pdf

And so the question, when we implement/deploy SAP Access Control on S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, what is the "best practice recommendation from SAP" regarding the Client where Access Control is operating, should we operate Access Control on Client 000, or, should we operate Access Control in another Client, in a similar as is described for operating Process Control in a different Client 1505255 - Transfer client-specific Customizing for PC/RM 10.0 - SAP for Me

The worry is, if we operate Access Control in Client 000 on S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, since SAP ECS own Client 000, operating Access Control in 000, due to the Segregation of Duties between ECS and the Customer, operating Access Control could become difficult and complex.

What is the recommendation from SAP and other people's experience ?

Thanks and best regards,

Andy Silvey.

tagging:

@ElyasAhmed 

@T_Frenehard

entry.title: Re: The requested lookup key was not found in any active...
entry.content:
My program code has been modified as follows and it can be used normally now
Those who have questions can refer to it

using System;
using SAPbouiCOM.Framework;

namespace Attribute2
{
    class Program
    {
        public static SAPbouiCOM.Application SBO_Application;
        public static SAPbobsCOM.Company oCompany;
        public static string SelectedItemCode = "";

        [STAThread]
        static void Main(string[] args)
        {
            try
            {
                // ✅ 初始化 UI 应用程序（必须）Initialize the UI application (mandatory)
                Application app = args.Length < 1 ? new Application() : new Application(args[0]);

                // ✅ 获取 UI 应用对象 Obtain the UI application object
                SBO_Application = Application.SBO_Application;

                // ✅ 注册 AppEvent（关闭等）Register for AppEvent (close, etc.)
                SBO_Application.AppEvent += new SAPbouiCOM._IApplicationEvents_AppEventEventHandler(SBO_Application_AppEvent);

                // ✅ 获取 DI 公司对象 Obtain the object of DI Company
                oCompany = (SAPbobsCOM.Company)SBO_Application.Company.GetDICompany();

                if (!oCompany.Connected)
                {
                    SBO_Application.StatusBar.SetText("连接失败：无法获取 DI Company-Connection failure: DI Company cannot be obtained", SAPbouiCOM.BoMessageTime.bmt_Short, SAPbouiCOM.BoStatusBarMessageType.smt_Error);
                    return;
                }

                SBO_Application.StatusBar.SetText("连接成功！DI Company 已准备好-Connection successful! DI Company is ready", SAPbouiCOM.BoMessageTime.bmt_Short, SAPbouiCOM.BoStatusBarMessageType.smt_Success);

                // ✅ 添加菜单 Add menu
                Menu myMenu = new Menu();
                myMenu.AddMenuItems();

                // ✅ 注册菜单事件处理器 Register the menu event handler
                app.RegisterMenuEventHandler(myMenu.SBO_Application_MenuEvent);

                // ✅ 初始化自定义逻辑（事件监听、表单注册等） Initialize custom logic (event listening, form registration, etc.)
                setAttribute setA = new setAttribute(SBO_Application, oCompany);
                setA.Initialize();

                // ✅ 进入主循环 Enter the main cycle
                app.Run();
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                System.Windows.Forms.MessageBox.Show("启动失败：" + ex.Message + "\n" + ex.StackTrace);
            }
        }

        static void SBO_Application_AppEvent(SAPbouiCOM.BoAppEventTypes EventType)
        {
            if (EventType == SAPbouiCOM.BoAppEventTypes.aet_ShutDown)
            {
                System.Windows.Forms.Application.Exit();
            }
        }
    }
}

entry.title: Re: In SuccessFactors PMGM, I would like to hide the old...

Hello 

Hope you're doing well!

The forms displayed under the "Team Overview" tab are controlled by the "Start of Review" setting within the route map. If your older form templates have their own associated route maps, you can try disabling the "Start of Review" setting in those route maps. After making this change, please check if it resolves the issue.

Thanks,

Amaan

entry.title: Error in Customer Master Migration in Migrate your Data
entry.content:
Hi all,

I encountered this error while doing the simulation of the customer master migration in the Migrate Your Data Application.

"Address with external number CCPP01KEY for partner CCPP10010 already exists"

Message Class R1, Message Number 658

I've checked the addresses in my template for both the main address in "General Data" and the additional address in the "Additional Addresses" Worksheet is different. However, the system kept throwing me with the same error message.

This is the main address.

And this is the additional address which is very different.

May I ask how can I resolved this error since the error is not relevant to the provided data at all?

Thank you for your advice on this.

Dan

entry.title: Re: Document Due Notification

entry.title: Digitale Adoption mit WalkMe in der Praxis: Erfahrungen der Deutschen Telekom Einführung in Digital
entry.content:
Die Community Session der SAP Change und Training Community bot am 22.05. einen Einblick in die Nutzung von Digital Adoption Plattformen, insbesondere WalkMe. Laura Hartmann und Dennis Buchholz von der Deutschen Telekom stellten ihre Erfahrungen mit WalkMe vor.

Was ist WalkMe?

Nach einem kurzen Checkin startete Laura und ging zunächst auf WalkMe ein:

WalkMe ist eine Digital Adoption Plattform (DAP), die wie eine interaktive Glasscheibe über jede Software-Anwendung gelegt werden kann und Nutzer:innen durch kontextsensitive Hilfen, Schritt-für-Schritt-Anleitungen und automatisierte Workflows hilft, komplexe Systeme effizienter zu nutzen. Die Plattform ist einem GPS-System ähnlich, das den Weg von A nach B weist. Die Plattform erkennt, wo sich der Nutzer:innen befinden und bietet kontextbezogene Hilfestellung. Zudem gibt es Reporting zum Status der User Adoption und verschiedene Möglichkeiten der Automation. Laura erklärte zunächst den Ansatz der Telekom rund um digital Adoption.

Der Lean Adoption Ansatz der Telekom

Die Telekom MMS verwendet einen strukturierten Ansatz für Digital Adoption Projekte, der vom Lean Startup-Konzept inspiriert wurde. Dieser "Lean Adoption"-Prozess besteht aus folgenden Phasen und Aktionen:

Ergänzt wird dieser Prozess durch zwei wichtige Komponenten:

  • Enablement auf Kundenseite
  • Aufbau eines Centers of Excellence für den unternehmensweiten Austausch

Erfolgsfaktoren für Digital Adoption Projekte

Die Telekom hat in sechs Jahren WalkMe-Implementierungen wesentliche Erfolgsfaktoren identifiziert:

Orientierung entlang der User Journey statt purer Tool-Fokus

  • Mailings vor Go-live an Stakeholder und Mitarbeitende
  • Intranet-Beiträge und User Group Meetings
  • WalkMe Days als Bootcamp-Format für den Austausch
  • Videobotschaften von Führungskräften zur Unterstützung

 Klare Messbare Ziele, KPIs und Nutzen

  • Klare Definition von KPIs und Business Objectives
  • Nachverfolgung der Nutzungsdaten
  • Regelmäßige Insight Sessions nach Implementierung
  • Kontinuierliche Anpassung an Nutzerverhalten

Berücksichtigung des Reifegrads der Organisation und klare Verantwortungen

  • Start mit kleinen Projekten und Success Stories
  • Interne Bewerbung der Erfolge
  • Berücksichtigung der Fehlerkultur
  • Klare Zuständigkeiten und Aufbau eines Centers of Excellence
  • Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs)

WalkMe Praxisbeispiele aus und in der Telekom

Die Telekom setzt WalkMe in ganz verschiedenen Bereichen ein:

Salesforce CRM

WalkMe unterstützt den Vertrieb beim Wechsel auf die neue CRM-Plattform durch In-App-Guides und Onboarding, reduziert Schulungsaufwand und fördert Akzeptanz.  

Intranet (YaM)

 

WalkMe ermöglichte Self-Service im Intranet in 6 Sprachen – vom Onboarding bis zur Hilfestellung für die Seitenerstellung.

Ticketing-Tool SKSWMS

 

WalkMe erleichtert Hotline-Mitarbeitenden die Ticketbearbeitung mit kontextuellen Anleitungen & Automatisierungen – für schnellere Bearbeitung und weniger Fehler.  ROI von über 300% bei 400 Nutzern.

Prozessautomatisierung

 

WalkMe spart produktive Minuten durch Prozessautomatisierung in 8 Anwendungen innerhalb der DT ISP.  

Konkreter Einsatz von WalkMe im Ticketing System

Dennis Buchholz demonstrierte anhand des Ticketing-Systems SKSWMS verschiedene WalkMe-Komponenten.

Die Mitarbeitenden der Telekom Hotline standen vor einer großen Herausforderung in ihrem Arbeitsalltag. Sie mussten mit einem veralteten und komplexen System arbeiten, ständig zwischen verschiedenen Anwendungen wechseln und benötigten umfangreiches Fachwissen, um auf unterschiedliche Kundenanliegen schnell reagieren zu können.

Besonders betroffen waren 400 Hotline-Mitarbeitende, deren Arbeit durch eine besonders hohe Komplexität gekennzeichnet war.

Um diese Situation zu verbessern, wurden verschiedene WalkMe-Lösungen eingesetzt. Direkt im System wurden Schritt-für-Schritt-Hilfen implementiert, die die Mitarbeitenden durch die Prozesse führten.

Wiederkehrende Aufgaben wurden durch Automatisierungen stark vereinfacht. Für schnelle Hilfestellung sorgten "Smart Tipps" und gezielte Hinweise an den richtigen Stellen. Besonders wirksam war ein Chatbot mit API-Anbindung, der direkte Systemaktionen ausführen konnte.

Die Ergebnisse dieser Maßnahmen waren laut Dennis beeindruckend. Die Mitarbeitenden zeigten sich deutlich zufriedener, da sie ihre Aufgaben nun einfacher und korrekter bearbeiten konnten.

Das Unternehmen erzielte einen Return on Investment von über 300% durch die gezielte Entlastung der Mitarbeitenden. Allein durch die automatisierte Erstellung von Briefen konnten Kosten im fünfstelligen Bereich eingespart werden. Diese Implementierung zeigt, wie digitale Adoption-Lösungen den Arbeitsalltag spürbar verbessern und gleichzeitig wirtschaftliche Vorteile bringen können.

Anbei nochmals die verschiedenen Elemente im Detail, die eingesetzt wurden:

Launcher

  • Hinzufügen von Buttons zur Oberfläche
  • Zentrale Platzierung häufig genutzter Links

WalkMe Menu

  • Schneller Zugriff auf häufig genutzte Inhalte
  • Zentrale Suche für Prozessdokumentationen (Ressourcen)
  • Zugang zu Onboarding Inhalten
  • Ablageort für Smart Walkthrus und Shuttles (Verlinkungen zu anderen Webseiten oder Tools)

Smart Walk-Thrus

  • Schritt-für-Schritt-Anleitungen durch Prozesse
  • Automatisches Ausfüllen von Formularen
  • Prüfung von Rahmenbedingungen (z.B. Kontingente)

Smart Tips

  • Kontextbezogene Informationen direkt in der Anwendung
  • Vermeidung der Suche in externen Dokumentationen

Action Bot

  • Chatbot mit Aktionsfähigkeiten
  • Abfrage wichtiger Informationen
  • API-Anbindung an Unternehmensanwendungen und Daten
  • Automatisierung von Prozessen (z.B. Brieferstellung)

Technische Integration

Walk Me lässt sich vergleichsweise einfach in bestehende Systeme integrieren:

  • Registrierung im API Gateway des Unternehmens
  • Anmeldung an relevanten API-Endpunkten
  • Low-Code-Ansatz für API-Konfiguration
  • Unterstützung verschiedener Umgebungen (Test, QA, Produktion)

Inspirierende Aussagen von Laura und Dennis

Anbei einige Aussagen der beiden die bei mir hängen geblieben sind:

  • "Es reicht nicht, einfach nur ein Tool einzuführen - Menschen brauchen Orientierung entlang ihres Arbeitstages."
  • "Digital Adoption braucht immer Pain Points und einen messbaren Nutzen, um das Maximum herauszuholen."
  • "Was ich an WalkMe besonders schätze, ist die Einfachheit - wir können als Fachbereich selbst publizieren ohne IT-Beteiligung."
  • „Man kann Leute einfach dran setzen und es ist ziemlich intuitiv - man muss nicht monatelang studieren, bevor man es anwenden kann."
  • "Wir nehmen wirklich die Notwendigkeit raus, die Leute immer wieder nachzuschulen, weil wir das einfach durch WalkMe abfangen können."
  • „Wenn ich etwas zu sagen hätte und Budget hätte, hätte ich auch WalkMe gekauft 😊“

Tipps für die Praxis in Digital Adoption Projekten

Anbei eine Zusammenfassung verschiedener Tipps, die während der Session geteilt wurden. 

  1. Klar definierte Ziele setzen: Vor der Implementierung messbare KPIs und Business Objectives festlegen.
  2. Iterativ vorgehen: Früh lernen statt lange planen - kleine Erfolge erzielen und darauf aufbauen.
  3. Ressourcen für Lifecycle-Management einplanen: Ausreichend Kapazitäten für kontinuierliche Anpassung und Aktualisierung der Inhalte bereitstellen.
  4. Kommunikation priorisieren: Nutzer:innen durch gezielte Kommunikation vor, während und nach der Einführung begleiten.
  5. User-Feedback einbeziehen: Nutzungsdaten analysieren und für kontinuierliche Verbesserung nutzen.

 Laura fasste Ihre Lernerfahrungen auch nochmals in einer Folie zusammen - anbei der Screenshot davon:

 

Zusammenfassung der Q&A-Session am Ende

API-Integration

Auf die Frage zur API-Anbindung erklärte Dennis Buchholz, dass Walk Me relativ einfach über ein API Gateway angebunden werden kann. Nach einmaliger Registrierung und Erhalt der Credentials ist hauptsächlich die Anbindung an die jeweiligen Endpunkte erforderlich. Der Low-Code-Ansatz macht die Implementierung benutzerfreundlich – so dass nach kurzer Einarbeitung erste Ergebnisse erzielt werden können.

KI-Features

Bezüglich KI-Funktionalitäten wurde erwähnt, dass WalkMe bereits verschiedene Features wie eigene Assistenten und Vorschläge für die nächsten sinnvollen Aktionen bietet. Auf der SAPPHIRE- wurden weitere KI-Integrationen vorgestellt wie die Integration in SAP Joule (sehr spannend!).

Systemanforderungen und Anwendungsbereiche

WalkMe funktioniert nicht nur in Browsern, sondern auch mit Desktop-Anwendungen wie SAP ECC und auf mobilen Geräten. 

SAP SuccessFactors-Integration von WalkMe

Für SuccessFactors-Module wird WalkMe als zukunftsweisende Lösung empfohlen, besonders wenn SAP Enable Now noch nicht im Einsatz ist. Inhalte, die mit SAP Enable Now als Standardcontent bereit gestellt wurden, werden bereits in WalkMe migriert. Auch gibt es neue Standard-Inhalte in verschiedenen Lösungen wie SuccessFactors.

Wartungsaufwand 

Der technische Nachbearbeitungsaufwand ist dank des intuitiven Editors gering. WalkMe's patentierte DeepUI-Technologie erkennt automatisch HTML-Strukturen und passt sich bei Änderungen an, sodass Inhalte bei kleineren Updates nicht sofort brechen.

Langzeitnutzung & Life-Cycle Management

Die Langzeitnutzung geht deutlich über die anfängliche Einführungsphase hinaus. Dennis hat über 600 WalkMe-Elemente erstellt und pflegt diese kontinuierlich. Entscheidend ist, ausreichend Ressourcen für die laufende Wartung und Anpassung an neue Releases einzuplanen. Dies ist ein kritischer Erfolgsfaktor für digitale Adoption. 

Test- und Produktiv-Umgebungen

Für verschiedene Systemumgebungen bietet WalkMe flexible Lösungen: Man kann unterschiedliche Oberflächen für Test-, QA- und Produktivumgebungen anlegen. Bei Kunden mit nur einem Produktivsystem kann mit Segmentierungen gearbeitet werden, sodass Inhalte zunächst nur für bestimmte Nutzergruppen sichtbar sind.

Zusammenfassung & Recap

Die Implementierung von Digital Adoption Plattformen wie WalkMe erfordert mehr als nur technisches Know-how. Erfolgskritisch ist ein strukturierter Ansatz, der die Nutzer:innen in den Mittelpunkt stellt. Die Deutsche Telekom hat mit ihrem "Lean Adoption"-Ansatz einen Weg gefunden, Digital Adoption Projekte effizient und wirkungsvoll umzusetzen.

Die vorgestellten Praxisbeispiele zeigen, dass WalkMe nicht nur bei der initialen Einführung von Software wertvolle Dienste leistet, sondern auch langfristig zur Effizienzsteigerung und Prozessoptimierung beiträgt.  Wir werden das Thema aus verschiedenen Perspektiven weiter beleuchten – gewünscht war u.a. auch die Erfahrung bzgl. der Integration in SAP Systeme sowie die Bedeutung für SAP Enable Now Kunden. 

Die nächsten Erfahrungsberichte und viele weitere Inspirationen und Austausch-Möglichkeiten gibt es bereits im SAP Learning und Adoption Forum am 24/25.06.  

WalkMe bietet auch weitere Einblicke in ihrem "WalkMe on Tour Event" in Frankfurt am 11. Juni. 

Auch möchten wir Euch gerne zur nächsten virtuellen Kaffee-Ecke einladen:

Die Rolle von Führungskräften im Lernen und Change Management in SAP Projekten - 17. Juli - 15 Uhr – virtuell, mit Melanie Zucker - Director Project Management - Change | TRANS4RM Program
adidas. Melanie hat über 30 Jahre Erfahrungen bei adidas in den Bereichen Finanzen, Supply Chain, Produktentwicklung und IT, Prozess- und Veränderungsmanagement und auch großen SAP Transformationen. In der Kaffee-Ecke reflektiert sie die Rolle von Führungskräften. Einbindung, Befähigung, Alignment, Kommunikation, Ziele und Erwartungen aber auch die eigene Führungsrolle sind u.a. Themen. 

Wir freuen uns über Eure Fragen oder Kommentare hier unter dem Blog.

entry.title: Re: Credit checks available in SAP Credit Management (FSCM)

Hello @janna_kor ,

External breakpoint should stop. Make sure it is set for the same user who trigger the credit check.
Session breakpoints are not stopping, because the credit check is technically called in an other session.

Edina

entry.title: Re: Sales order header text to billing doc. header text n...
entry.content:
Did you use Transaction VOTXN or SPRO: Sales and Distribution > Basic Functions > Text Control > Define Text Types to

    Define Text Types (Text IDs) for Sales Order and Billing Document.
    Assign Text Types to Text Procedures: Header level of Sales Order and Billing
    Assign Text Procedures to Document Types: E.g., OR for sales order, F2 for billing
  2. Set Up Text Copying Rules
    Use "Access Sequence" to define how text should be copied.
    Under "Text Determination Procedure Assignment":
    Maintain copying rules from Sales Order to Billing.

entry.title: Re: Problem for creation of cost element category 31
entry.content:
Hello Steffen, the solution suggested above with T-code OB_GLACC14 works fine. Let me just provide some additional information.

The new GL account (as Secondary Cost Element) need to be created in FSP0. Then with OB_GLACC14 (Mass change), you select your Controlling Area and you add your Cost Element Category 31.

This Cost Element will appear in FS00 for all Company Code in the Controlling Area without being link to any. Nice trick.

Then you can use in T-code OKG2 for WIP or C+VF configuration. This Cost Element will be accepted as "Technical RA Cost Element".

Hope it will help other to save time.

In the manual implementation guide, it's the other way around just to let you know.

entry.title: Strengthening Your SAP CPQ Deployment with Continuous Quality Checks
entry.content:
Are you implementing SAP CPQ (Configure, Price, Quote) or managing its day-to-day operations? In both scenarios, ensuring the quality and stability of your solution is vital. SAP Enterprise Support offers continuous quality checks to help you achieve this. Continuous quality checks are included as part of your SAP Enterprise Support cloud edition maintenance contract, embedded in your SAP CPQ subscription at no extra cost.

SAP CPQ is a cloud solution that enables businesses to streamline and optimize their sales processes. It allows sales teams to accurately configure complex products, generate error-free quotes, and automate approvals, all while ensuring compliance with business rules and improving overall sales efficiency. It can empower your sales team and partners to offer complex product configurations, optimized pricing, and great-looking proposals. 

Below is the continuous quality check (CQC) available to strengthen your SAP CPQ deployment, tailored to your specific implementation phase and needs:

 

Service

Benefit

When to request

CQC for Deployment Readiness for SAP Configure, Price, Quote (CPQ)

Ensures operational efficiency and readiness by performing a detailed analysis of your SAP CPQ, either before go-live or as a "health check" for your productive systems.

(Highly recommended) 
Realize phase - Two months prior to go live 

(Proactive) Run phase 

Additional Resources and Support:

To learn more about SAP CPQ onboarding and introduction, visit the e-learnings in our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition value map*.

*To access the link and for ongoing support and actionable guidance tailored to your unique SAP journey, we invite you to join and explore SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps. 

Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps 

Beyond CQC services, SAP Enterprise Support offers additional resources to help you drive innovation. SAP Enterprise Support value maps provide expert-led resources, tailored recommendations, and advisory services - all included in your SAP cloud subscription. Get started today: 

  • One-time sign up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, using a valid S-User ID (please allow up to 2 hours for processing) 
  • Access SAP Enterprise Support value maps for personalized guidance 
  • Request a call with our solution area experts for tailored advice 

Value Maps are part of SAP Enterprise Support commitment to your success. Visit the SAP Enterprise Support value maps landing page to explore our range of topics and start engaging with us today.

About the author: 

  • Icy Huang is a Focus Advisor for SAP Enterprise Support value maps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Based in Singapore, Icy has been with SAP since 2007, taking on diverse roles that have largely centered around customer engagement and success across the organization. Now she focuses on helping business accelerate the adoption of SAP S/4HANA Cloud solutions with value map content and SAP Enterprise Support offerings.

Have questions, comments or feedback? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @IcyHuang

entry.title: OData response time reduced significantly after model changes and fastens up after 8-9 hours
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing a strange issue.

I have built an unmanaged RAP application with 8 entities in SAP on-prem system where all of them are related to each other via associations and compositions. Each entity on an average are having around 25-30 fields. I have created cds, behavior, metadata extensions, service definition and OData V2 UI service binding.

All works good, but the issue is, whenever I change any thing in my model like field logic, or add additional field etc., OData response slows down significantly.

It takes around 35 - 40 seconds to load metadata sometimes it takes even minutes. It again fastens up after 8 - 9 hours and loads in 600 - 800ms.

I tried doing everything including

invalidating global cache

invalidating client cache

invalidating metadata cache

I even tried running report /UI5/UPD_ODATA_METADATA_CACHE

Nothing helps

I would be happy to know what is the potential cause for this and how to resolve

 

metadata loads in 670ms without any modifications.

 

Same service taking 35 seconds after field modification

Hello, 

We have made a software that uses ServiceLayer to create Invoice from Delivery or SalesOrder. But now, a new customer reported that he got a NotFound error when trying to create an Invoice from Delivery, and here is the http request:

POST https://example.com/sl/b1s/v1/Invoices

Content is:

{
  "CardCode": "C00001",
  "DocEntry": 0,
  "DocObjectCode": 0,
  "DocNum": 0,
  "TrackingNumber": "123456789123",
  "PickRemark": "",
  "DocumentLines": [
    {
      "BaseEntry": 33,
      "LineNum": 0,
      "BaseLine": 0,

entry.title: Re: HeaderInfo - Transalate or change Text 'Object Inform...
entry.content:
💡 Fiori Elements V4 :

If you want to hide the Header Content section in an Object Page, just add the following setting to your manifest.json:

"editableHeaderContent": false

This disables editing for the header area and effectively hides it from the UI.
Simple, clean, and effective! 🚀

Sample Code (Source)
 
"sap.ui5": {
        ...
        "routing": {
                ...
                {
                    "pattern": "SalesOrderManage({key})/_Item({key2}):?query:",
                    "name": "SalesOrderItemObjectPage",
                    "target": "SalesOrderItemObjectPage"
                }
            ],
            "targets": {
                "SalesOrderItemObjectPage": {
                    "type": "Component",
                    "id": "SalesOrderItemObjectPage",
                    "name": "sap.fe.templates.ObjectPage",
                    "options": {
                        "settings": {
                            "contextPath": "/SalesOrderItem",
                            "editableHeaderContent": false,
                            ...
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

entry.title: Calling a Remote Action from Event Handler in CAP Java
entry.content:
Hello together,

does anyone have an example or guidance on how to call a remote action from an event handler in CAP Java?

In Node.js, calling a remote action is quite straightforward, as shown in the official documentation:
👉 https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/core-services#consuming-services

However, in Java, I understand that it involves working with CQN and using the remote service proxy:
👉 https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/java/cqn-services/remote-services

Unfortunately, I haven’t found a concrete example yet. For instance, the SAP Cloud CAP Java samples don't seem to demonstrate calling a remote action specifically.

Has anyone implemented this or could share a snippet or an approach for calling a remote-bound action from within a CAP Java event handler?

Thanks a lot in advance and best regards,

Julian

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Which notification to use when – Workflow, Situation Handling, KPIs,
entry.content:
@StephanHeinberg Yes...  well observed... and there are good reasons for that...

1. With SAP S/4HANA we try to smooth the journey where we can in.  To minimize disruption, existing classic workflows are still supported in a system conversion scenario. 

2. My Inbox is one of very few SAP Fiori apps that can be used in both ECC and SAP S/4HANA.

3. Many of the Flexible Workflows that were written for SAP S/4HANA provide newly designed sub-tasks that work with My Inbox, support mobile devices, and lift the UI design overall. These can also be federated up to SAP Task Center.

4. SAP Task Center is considered the go-to unified inbox across multiple SAP Cloud solutions, and is definitely worth considering.

entry.title: Re: Can anyone link me to Hana DB SQL queries documentati...
entry.content:
SQL dialect differs between different databases of different vendors. SAP HANA's compliance with the mandatory features of the ANSI SQL/2016 standard: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/4fe29514fd584807ac9f2a04f6754767/09940a1ed38c4ceb80770632997b286b.html?locale=en-US

In SAP HANA SQL, the DUMMY table is a special one-row, one-column table that is often used when a query requires a FROM clause but no actual table data is needed. It is similar to the DUAL table in Oracle.

Here are some common use cases for the DUMMY table:

  1. Selecting a Constant Value: When you want to return a constant value or perform a calculation that doesn't involve any table data, you can use the DUMMY table.

     
     
SELECT 'Hello, World!' AS greeting FROM DUMMY;

  • Calling Functions: When you need to call a function that doesn't require any table data.

     
     
SELECT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP AS now FROM DUMMY;
